title: Factory Status Taxonomy
description: Canonical lifecycle classification of the divergent per-service status vocabularies (PFactory board states, AIFactory backlog/in_progress/ai_review/human_review/done, TFactory triaged/passed/...). The cockpit and any consumer maps an arbitrary status string to ONE lifecycle state by EXACT, normalized-token match (never loose substring containment, which previously made 'ready' match 'already' and 'fail' match a status merely containing those letters). This document mirrors CFactory's canonical status_taxonomy.py token sets (apps/backend/cfactory/status_taxonomy.py, #114) so the hub is the single source of truth from which the shared models are generated.

normalization.description: How a raw status string is reduced to tokens before matching: lowercased, stripped, then split on whitespace, '_' and '-'. A classification matches when ANY resulting token is in the relevant token set. A compound status like 'human_review' or 'qa-approved' yields its parts (human, review / qa, approved).

failed.description: Failure / rejection / abort markers. A failed stage is terminal AND unhealthy.

done.description: Successful / completed-OK markers. A done stage is terminal AND healthy. 'skip'/'skipped' fold into healthy-terminal so a skipped stage is preserved as finished history during reconciliation.

review.description: Parked-for-review markers — finished executing, awaiting a human/AI decision. NOT terminal: the work item is still in flight.

queued.description: Queued / not-started markers — no agent attached yet. NOT terminal.

running.description: Actively-executing markers. Used to disambiguate a present-but-unknown status: a just-started agent often has no canonical mark yet and reads as running.

stuck.description: A stage explicitly marked stuck by an upstream (anomaly heuristic). Exposed via dedicated helpers (is_stuck / is_failure_or_stuck) so the anomaly detector keeps its reach without polluting the terminal check.

classification_rules.terminal: done OR failed (review/queued/running are NOT terminal; a terminal stage is preserved during reconciliation)
classification_rules.running_fallback: a present-but-unrecognized status reads as running (not terminal, not review, not queued); a falsy status is NOT running
classification_rules.active_fallback: a missing status reads as active (a just-started agent often has no mark yet); false for terminal, review, and queued statuses
